skill.name: software-architecture
skill.description: Create and maintain software architecture documentation with ARCHITECTURE.md, component detail files, and Mermaid diagrams for system structure, data flow, and key code entry points.
skill.summary: Document software architecture with structured markdown templates and Mermaid diagram patterns.
skill.author: Chemiseblanc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only markdown templates, inline code formatting, and Mermaid diagram examples. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection text, or malicious intent were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution patterns occur inside markdown fences, inline path examples, and template tables. They are documentation syntax and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are fenced markdown or Mermaid examples, not Ruby, shell, or another executable language. No command invocation API or generated script file is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections point to ordinary prose, Mermaid diagram labels, or markdown template text. No hashing, cipher selection, crypto library use, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain architecture documentation wording and Mermaid sample nodes. I found no cryptographic implementation or recommendation to use a weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are Documentation Context
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections come from architecture discovery guidance and Mermaid system diagrams. The skill asks the assistant to document code structure, not collect host, network, or identity data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is limited to architecture documentation templates and generic diagram examples. No instructions request operating system reconnaissance or data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Architecture documentation often falls behind code changes and becomes hard to trust. This skill provides structured markdown templates and Mermaid diagram guidance for current, navigable system documentation.
actual_capabilities: Creates a root ARCHITECTURE.md structure with overview, diagrams, entry points, abstractions, testing, and detail links.
Defines component detail document sections for docs/*.md files.
Selects suitable Mermaid diagram types for context, container, component, sequence, flowchart, ER, and class views.
Documents key entry points, important abstractions, code organization, and testing locations.
Provides maintenance triggers for updating architecture documentation after structural code changes.
limitations: Requires access to the project files to produce accurate architecture references.
Does not validate Mermaid rendering across every documentation platform.
Does not replace a formal architecture review by senior engineers.
Documentation can become stale if teams do not update it during code changes.

use_cases.title: Create Initial Architecture Docs
use_cases.description: Generate a practical ARCHITECTURE.md and component documentation plan for an existing repository.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams onboarding to a codebase

use_cases.title: Update Docs After Refactoring
use_cases.description: Refresh diagrams, entry point tables, and component links after meaningful structural changes.

use_cases.title: Explain System Design During Reviews
use_cases.description: Produce concise diagrams and tables that help reviewers understand system boundaries and dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Start Architecture Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a repository with no architecture docs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create initial architecture documentation for this repository. Add an ARCHITECTURE.md outline, identify key entry points, and suggest component docs under docs/.

prompt_templates.title: Document One Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Document the main responsibilities, key files, abstractions, dependencies, and test coverage for the component I specify.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Architecture Diagrams
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this codebase structure and recommend the most useful Mermaid diagrams for system context, components, data flow, and request flow.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh Architecture After Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced maintenance after a refactor or feature branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the current architecture documentation with the recent structural changes. Update affected diagrams, entry points, abstractions, and component doc links.

output_examples.input: A backend service has API handlers, a service layer, repositories, background workers, and tests.
output_examples.output: A concise architecture document with a system overview, Mermaid container diagram, entry point table, abstraction table, testing section, and links to component detail docs.

output_examples.input: A team refactored authentication into a new module and changed several entry points.
output_examples.output: Updated architecture notes that add the authentication component, adjust request flow diagrams, and refresh file references for handlers, services, and tests.

output_examples.input: A reviewer needs to understand how data moves through a pipeline.
output_examples.output: A focused data flow explanation with a Mermaid flowchart recommendation and a short list of files that implement each stage.

best_practices: Keep diagrams small and expand them only when they improve understanding.
Use relative file paths so readers can navigate directly from documentation to code.
Update architecture docs in the same change that alters system structure.
anti_patterns: Creating diagrams that copy every file or class without explaining design intent.
Documenting planned architecture as if it already exists in the codebase.
Leaving ARCHITECTURE.md disconnected from component docs and test locations.

faq.question: What files does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It focuses on ARCHITECTURE.md and component detail files under docs/*.md.

faq.question: Does it generate Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: It provides Mermaid diagram patterns and recommends diagram types for architecture documentation.

faq.question: Can it document an existing codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect project structure and produce documentation based on actual files and abstractions.

faq.question: Is this only for large systems?
faq.answer: No. It encourages small diagrams and lightweight documentation for projects of many sizes.

faq.question: How should teams keep the documentation current?
faq.answer: Update diagrams, entry points, and component docs when adding components, refactoring, or changing dependencies.

faq.question: Does it perform security analysis?
faq.answer: No. It documents architecture and can note security-relevant components, but it is not a security audit tool.
